ddls:
CREATE TABLE "specimen_collections" (
  "collection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each specimen collection entry */,
  "specimen_collection_date" TEXT /* Date when the specimen was collected */,
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the disease table */,
  "new_confirmed_cases" INTEGER /* Number of new confirmed cases reported on the collection date */,
  "cumulative_confirmed_cases" INTEGER /* Total number of confirmed cases by the collection date */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the record */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., public health agency, research institution) */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the location where the specimen was collected */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the location where the specimen was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_specimen_collections_disease_id FOREIGN KEY ("disease_id") REFERENCES diseases ("disease_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "race_ethnicity" TEXT /* Race and ethnicity category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the demographic category */,
  "population_size" INTEGER /* Estimated population size of the demographic category */,
  "age_distribution" TEXT /* Age distribution of the demographic category (e.g., 20-30, 30-40) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographic_cases" (
  "demographic_case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic case entry */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the demographics table */,
  "collection_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the specimen collections table */,
  "new_cases" INTEGER /* Number of new cases for the demographic category on the collection date */,
  "cumulative_cases" INTEGER /* Total number of cases for the demographic category by the collection date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_cases_demographic_id FOREIGN KEY ("demographic_id") REFERENCES demographics ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_cases_collection_id FOREIGN KEY ("collection_id") REFERENCES specimen_collections ("collection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "diseases" (
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disease entry */,
  "disease_name" TEXT /* Name of the disease */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the disease */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disease_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update entry */,
  "collection_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the specimen collection entry being updated */,
  "update_type" TEXT /* Type of update (e.g., new cases, revised figures) */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date of the update */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes regarding the update */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who made the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_updates_collection_id FOREIGN KEY ("collection_id") REFERENCES specimen_collections ("collection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user entry */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., public health official, data analyst, researcher) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report entry */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data contained in the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report request entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report requested */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date the report was requested */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the reports table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_requests_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)
